FT_IC1.evidence: Uses U.S. ABCD Study data from Year 3 (2019-2021), N=9989 early adolescents

FT_IC2.evidence: Paper measures cyberbullying victimization only; does not measure a specific smartphone or social media variable. Cyberbullying is mentioned but no phone/SM exposure variable is included (E6 applies: cyberbullying only without separately-measured phone/SM variable)

FT_IC3.evidence: No association test linking a smartphone/SM variable to a non-SM outcome. The paper only tests transgender identity (IV) → cyberbullying (DV), with no phone/SM variable involved

FT_IC4.evidence: FT_IC2 NOT_MET means no phone/SM variable to extract numeric from

FT_IC5.evidence: Original empirical ABCD analysis; cross-sectional logistic regression with demographic IV and cyberbullying outcome

FT_IC6.evidence: Journal article (Academic Pediatrics 2025); no indication of duplicate

FT_EC7_reason: Paper fails FT_IC2 and FT_IC3; not evaluated for FT_EC7 since no phone/SM variable is present
notes: Exclusion criterion: FT_IC2 NOT_MET — cyberbullying victimization measured but no smartphone or social media exposure variable included in analysis

FT_IC1.evidence: Uses U.S. ABCD Study data from Year 3 (2019-2021), N=10,092 early adolescents

FT_IC2.evidence: Measures specific social media variables: platform-specific accounts (TikTok, YouTube, Instagram, Snapchat, Pinterest, Reddit, Facebook, Twitter), number of accounts, secret accounts, underage account status

FT_IC3.evidence: Chi-square and t-tests reported for demographic comparisons (sex, age, income) but no inferential test linking social media variables to a non-SM outcome. Only screen-to-screen or descriptive statistics (e.g., TikTok 67.1% of users). No hypothesis test with SM as predictor/outcome paired with non-SM outcome

FT_IC4.evidence: FT_IC3 NOT_MET means no association test to extract numerics from; only prevalence percentages and descriptive means reported

FT_IC5.evidence: Original empirical ABCD analysis; descriptive epidemiology of social media patterns

FT_IC6.evidence: Journal article (Academic Pediatrics 2025); no indication of duplicate

FT_EC7_reason: Paper fails FT_IC3 (no inferential test); FT_EC7 not applicable
notes: Exclusion criterion: FT_IC3 NOT_MET — descriptive analysis only (χ², t-tests for demographic comparisons) with no inferential test linking SM to a non-SM outcome

FT_IC1.evidence: Uses U.S. ABCD Study prospective data: Year 2 (2018-2020) baseline to Year 3 follow-up, N=8006 early adolescents

FT_IC2.evidence: Measures specific smartphone and social media variables: social media time (hours/day), texting (hours/day), video chatting (hours/day), TV/movies, videos, video games, internet use, total screen time. Phone-specific variables: texting and video chatting qualify as phone-mediated SM/communication

FT_IC3.evidence: Logistic regression analyses test prospective associations between 8 screen modalities and substance use experimentation outcomes. Example: social media (IV) → any substance use (DV); texting (IV) → alcohol/cannabis/nicotine (DVs); video chatting (IV) → cannabis/nicotine (DVs). Statistical tests include adjusted odds ratios with 95% CI and p-values

FT_IC4.evidence: Multiple numeric estimates reported: social media AOR 1.20 (95% CI 1.14-1.26, p<0.001 implied); texting AOR 1.18 (95% CI 1.12-1.24); video chatting AOR 1.09 (95% CI 1.03-1.16). All with confidence intervals and reported as significant

FT_IC5.evidence: Original empirical ABCD prospective/longitudinal analysis using logistic regression to test screen use (Year 2) predicting substance use experimentation (Year 3)

FT_IC6.evidence: Journal article (Drug and Alcohol Dependence 2025); no indication of duplicate

FT_EC7_reason: Paper reports phone/SM (social media, texting, video chatting) as independent variables predicting a non-SM outcome (substance use experimentation). Phone/SM is not the dependent variable; therefore FT_EC7 does not apply
notes: INCLUSION: All FT_IC1-6 MET. Phone/SM variables (social media, texting, video chatting) as prospective predictors of substance use outcomes with reported AORs and CIs. FT_EC7 not applicable (phone/SM is IV, not DV)
